channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: NFL player torches Steelers' Acrisure Stadium in poll: 'Smells like p---'
item.description: Acrisure Stadium, the Pittsburgh Steelers' home in the NFL, was ripped by an anonymous player in a poll when he was asked his least favorite road trip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Detroit Tigers sweep Astros to advance to second round of playoffs
item.description: Andy Ibáñez hit a tiebreaking three-run double in Detroit’s four-run eighth inning, and the Tigers swept the Houston Astros with a 5-2 victory in Game 2 of their AL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: Kristie Rieken, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sharks provide update on Celebrini’s health after scary moment
item.description: Macklin Celebrini was injured Tuesday night in the second period of the San Jose Sharks game against Utah Hockey Club at SAP Center.
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: Aces' quest to threepeat hits 2-0 hole vs Liberty while Sun and Lynx are split in WNBA playoffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Las Vegas Aces and Connecticut Sun head home with two very different feelings after the opening two games of their playoff semifinals series. The Aces are trying to save their season against New York while the Sun hope to close out the Minnesota Lynx.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG and DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bay Area News Group high school football predictions: Week 6, 2024
item.description: The guys pick 15 games across the Bay Area, including Los Gatos at Pittsburg, Archbishop Riordan at St. Ignatius, Saratoga at Monta Vista, St. Francis at Archbishop Mitty, and more.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: High School Boys Golf: Grand Junction shines in regionals, state tournaments set
item.description: This week, the 4A Region 4 tournament took place at Black Canyon Golf Course in Montrose. If we learned anything, it's that the Grand Junction Tigers are for real.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Jets' undisciplined play raises questions about head coach's leadership
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – We entered the season, and the sky was the limit for the Jets and expectations on the season. Four games in, and after a terrible 10-9 loss to the Broncos on Sunday, many things are being called into question.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Legendary Dodgers pitcher, broadcaster Fernando Valenzuela hospitalized
item.description: Dodgers broadcaster Fernando Valenzuela has been hospitalized ahead of the team's playoff run. The popular Spanish-language broadcaster and former Dodger star pitcher stepped away from his playcalling duties last week, though no reason was given at the time, according to the Orange County Register.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Are professional athletes playing too many games?
item.description: Fans, athletes and medical experts have been having discussions on the increasing demand for players starting from the time they first start playing, all the way up to the professional ranks.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, Fox 5

item.title: Utah Jazz veterans hope to lead young core
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - There are no less than ten players 23 years of age or younger currently on the Utah Jazz roster. So for veterans like Jordan Clarkson, they know their role as leaders is invaluable.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Pete Rose, legendary baseball player, dies at 83
item.description: Nicknamed "Charlie Hustle," the 17-time All Star holds the Major League Baseball record for the most career hits.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: (KRON) -- Baseball legend Pete Rose died Monday at the age of 83. Rose's complicated legacy includes being MLB's all-time hits leader but being banned from the league for gambling on games. He died Monday morning in his Las Vegas home, TMZ said. The outlet did not reveal a cause of death.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Reports: Pete Rose, baseball's all-time hits leader, dead at 83
item.description: LAS VEGAS — Pete Rose, one of Major League Baseball's most polarizing athletes and the game's all-time hits leader, has died at the age of 83, according to TMZ. Rose's agent, Ryan Fiterman, confirmed the news to TMZ and said, "the family is asking for privacy at this time.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Giants fire Farhan Zaidi, Buster Posey named as replacement
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The San Francisco Giants have parted ways with President of Baseball Operations Farhan Zaidi and named Buster Posey as his replacement.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Henry runs for 199 yards and Jackson has 3 TDs as Ravens rout previously unbeaten Bills 35-10
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Derrick Henry was off and running from the first time he touched the ball. There was no way the Buffalo Bills were stopping the Baltimore Ravens after that.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: College GameDay makes first-ever appearance at Berkeley
item.description: (KRON) -- Since taking its show on the road back in 1993, "College GameDay" has been a huge presence in the college football community by being known to travel across dozens of campuses across the country.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: White Sox wrap up record-breaking losing season with 9-5 win over playoff-bound Tigers
item.description: Lenyn Sosa hit a three-run homer in the third inning to give the record-breaking Chicago White Sox a five-run lead as they went on to beat the playoff-bound Detroit Tigers 9-5 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mayfield throws for 347 yards and accounts for three TDs to lead Bucs over Eagles 33-16
item.description: Baker Mayfield threw for 347 yards and two touchdowns, while Mike Evans became the Buccaneers' career scoring leader Sunday during Tampa Bay's 33-16 victory over the Philadelphia Eagles.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MLB regular season ends Sunday, playoffs to begin Oct. 1
item.description: The MLB post-season begins Oct. 1 with the Wild Card series. The end of the regular season marks an end to the Athletics' time in Oakland.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Utes offense struggles in 23-10 loss to Arizona
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The absence of Cam Rising finally caught up to the Utah football team, as the Utes offense produced just one touchdown and one field goal in a 23-10 loss to Arizona Saturday night at Rice-Eccles Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Padres postseason tickets on sale now
item.description: Tickets for 2024 postseason games went on sale to the public on Saturday, with access limited to residents in nearby geographical locations. The Padres still have two games left in the regular season, and fans can watch for free all weekend.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Utah Royals beat Racing Lousiville 1-0
item.description: In their third-to-last home game of the season, Utah Royals FC beat Racing Louisville 1-0 off of a goal from Ana Tejada. There were chances from both sides during the match, but only one shot made it into the back of the net on the night.
item.dc:creator: Stockton Mair, ABC 4

item.title: SJSU opponent forfeits volleyball match, lawsuit alleges player is transgender
item.description: (KRON) -- Boise State University's volleyball team forfeited a match against San Jose State, the school announced on Friday. The cancellation came after one San Jose State player said in a lawsuit that one of her teammates is transgender.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Taillon pitches 7 crisp innings as the Cubs blank the Reds 1-0
item.description: Jameson Taillon pitched seven crisp innings in his second straight win, and the Chicago Cubs beat the Cincinnati Reds 1-0 on Friday in the opener of their season-ending series.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What's next for the Oakland Coliseum now that the A's are gone?
item.description: (KRON) -- After 57 years at the Oakland Coliseum, the Oakland Athletics played their final game there on Thursday, giving the fans something to celebrate with a 3-2 win over the Texas Rangers in front of a sell-out crowd.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Shohei Ohtani heads to 1st postseason after Dodgers clinch NL West title with a 7-2 win over Padres
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani is headed to the postseason for the first time in his career after the Los Angeles Dodgers won the NL West on Thursday night by scoring five runs in the seventh inning in a 7-2 win over the second-place San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5
